items.headline: Iran rejects ceasefire deadline as oil disruption enters sixth week
items.body: Iran rejected proposals to reopen the Strait of Hormuz as the closure triggered the largest oil supply disruption in history. Nearly one billion barrels will be lost by month's end while diesel prices have more than doubled across Southeast Asia.

items.headline: China mediates as Pakistan and Afghanistan hold first talks after border clashes
items.body: Pakistan confirmed peace talks with Afghanistan's Taliban government in China after weeks of fighting that killed hundreds. Tensions peaked after a Pakistani airstrike at a Kabul treatment center that Afghanistan said killed more than 400 people.

items.headline: Somalia launches first offshore oil drilling with Turkish partnership
items.body: Somalia began its first offshore drilling campaign as Turkish vessel Cagri Bey arrived to drill the Curad-1 well. Officials said successful operations could boost resource-led growth and reshape regional energy dynamics.

meanwhile.text: 5,300-year-old Egyptian copper artifact reidentified as oldest known rotary metal drill in Nile Valley
Scientists trap infrared light in layer 40 nanometers thick—over 1,000 times thinner than human hair
New pill baxdrostat lowers high blood pressure nearly 10 mmHg in patients who don't respond to standard treatments
Young generation embracing conservative Christianity with radical views on race and gender drives US religious revival

es.headline: Spain employment surpasses 22 million for first time as services sector drives record hiring
es.body: Spain exceeded 22 million Social Security contributors for the first time in its history as Easter demand and services growth pushed March employment to an all-time high. The milestone marks a shift in a country long defined by stubborn joblessness, though regional unemployment gaps remain stark.

uk.headline: Britain scraps two-child benefit cap as welfare reform extends payments to additional children
uk.body: The two-child benefit cap ended, extending monthly payments to all children in low-income households after nine years of restriction. An estimated 450,000 children are projected to move above the poverty line as families receive additional support worth over £3,600 annually per child.

nl.headline: Netherlands doubles offshore wind tender to 2 GW in response to energy security concerns
nl.body: Government added second offshore wind site to 2026 tender with €3.9 billion support, reversing earlier budget cuts. Decision follows Strait of Hormuz closure and aims to accelerate energy independence amid supply disruptions.
